_provenance.status: DRAFT — pending author/supervisor sign-off AND the GPU-gated benign/adversary reach-floor validation (scripts/check_semantic_separation.py)
_provenance.method: LIBERO scene inspection: benchmark.get_benchmark_dict()['libero_spatial'](); per task, env.set_init_state(init_states[0]) then extract_object_poses(obs) / extract_ee_pos(obs). Run on CSB A5000 GPU 0 (MUJOCO_GL=egl); model-free, no OpenVLA load.
_provenance.benign_target_region: plate_1 (obj_of_interest[-1] for all three tasks; benign task = pick akita_black_bowl_1 -> place on plate_1)
_provenance.distractor_rationale: glazed_rim_porcelain_ramekin_1 (the ramekin): present in every scene, a distinct/unambiguous object type, named in the task language, and it PASSES the model-free separation guard (dist->goal > 0.15 m AND dist->ee > 0.05 m) for all three tasks. Alternatives considered: cookies_1 (passes, tighter margins ~0.16 m); akita_black_bowl_2 (the 2nd black bowl — thematically the strongest wrong-instance redirect, but FAILS separation on task_2 at 0.130 m < 0.15 m, so rejected for cross-task consistency).
_provenance.caveats: Distances are from the reset init-state (init_states[0]); the guard re-checks post-settle. The adv_instruction's realizability (a clean adversary rollout must reach the ramekin) is NOT yet validated — that is the GPU-gated reach-floor check. Do not treat this as accepted until both land.
